title: Sarah Paine - Why Putin and Xi can't escape geography
summary: Military historian Sarah Paine presents a two-thousand-year geopolitical framework dividing global powers into continental empires pursuing security through territorial conquest and maritime powers pursuing prosperity through trade. The analysis situates China and Russia within this continental paradigm and explores how industrialization and contemporary leaders like Putin operate within these geographical constraints.
writeup: Military historian Sarah Paine lays out a geopolitical framework spanning two thousand years that splits global powers into two types: continental empires that seek security through territorial conquest, and maritime powers that seek prosperity through trade. She places China and Russia in the continental category and traces how industrialization and leaders like Putin operate inside these geographic constraints. The takeaway is a structural reading of great power behavior, where the pursuit of land-based security and the pursuit of trade-based wealth produce distinct strategic logics that persist across centuries.
